title: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (Indiana Secretary of State)

communications.subject: Indiana Access to Public Records Act Request: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (Indiana Secretary of State)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Indiana Access to Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

From January 20, 2025 to the present:

1. All correspondence, emails, letters, and written communications between the agency and the U.S. Department of Justice, including but not limited to the Civil Rights Division, the Office of the Attorney General, and the Executive Office for Immigration Review, relating to voter registration data, voter files, voter rolls, or voter list maintenance.

2. All internal communications within the agency (including emails, memoranda, and briefing materials) discussing, referencing, or responsive to any DOJ request for voter registration data or any proposed or executed memorandum of understanding with the DOJ regarding voter data.

3. Any lists, spreadsheets, databases, or other records received from the DOJ identifying specific voters for removal, investigation, or further review.

4. All records relating to the engagement of any third-party contractor in connection with DOJ voter data requests or voter list maintenance activities conducted pursuant to any agreement with the DOJ.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Indiana Access to Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Any and all memoranda of understanding (MOUs), agreements, contracts, or similar instruments between the state and the U.S. Department of Justice relating to the provision of voter registration data, voter files, or voter roll information, from January 20, 2025 to the present.

2. Any draft, unsigned, proposed, or rejected versions of such agreements.

3. Any amendments, addenda, or modifications to such agreements.

This request includes but is not limited to any document described or labeled as a "memorandum of understanding" or contract relating to voter data, voter list maintenance, or voter roll verification.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

communications.subject: Acknowledgment Email - 2026-FOIA-00125
communications.communication:
Case No: 2026-FOIA-00125 
 
December 2, 2025 
Dear MuckRock News DEPT MR 197571: This email acknowledges receipt of your request, case tracking number 2026-FOIA-00125.  Your request is assigned a case tracking number based on the time it was received by FOIAXpress.  Please know that due to the government shutdown,FOIA requests were not processed from October 1, 2025 through November 12, 2025.  The government reopened on November 13,2025.  We appreciate your patience as weresume FOIA case processing. 
The FOIA is centrally processed at the Social Security Administration (SSA) within the Office of Privacy and Disclosure with a subset of requests – requests for copies of decedents’ original SS-5 and Numident records – processed by the Office of Central Operations, Division of Earnings and Business Services.  Please know that FOIA requests are categorized as either simple or complex, depending on the nature of the request and the estimated processing time.  For most non-expedited simple requests, we make a determination about release of the record(s) requested within 20 business days.  We will place into a complex processing queue any request that cannot be completed within 20 business days due to unusual circumstances. 

If you need to correspond with us regarding your request, you may leave a voicemail by calling 410-965-1727 or you may send email correspondence to our FOIA Public Lia ison at FOIA.Public.Liaison@ssa.gov .  When you email this mailbox, we encourage you to limit the amount of personally identifiable information you provide in your email correspondence.  While SSA operates within a secure network, we have no control of the data we receive while it is in transit to or from our FOIA Public Liaison mailbox.  Please include your case tracking number in your email. Sincerely, 
The FOIA and Privacy Act Team 
SSA

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request #2026-FOIA-00125 - Status
communications.communication:
Dear Sir or Madam, 

The records you seekrequire the need for consultation with multiple components of the agency.  Because of these unusual circumstances, weneed to extend the time limit to respond to your request. See 5U.S.C. § 552(a)(6)(B)(iii). 
No later than January 26, 2026, you will receive further correspondence from us. In accordance with 5 U.S.C. §552(a)(6)(B)(ii), because we require more than ten additional working days toprocess your request, you have the opportunity to:  (1) modify or narrowthe scope of your request so that it can be processed within the required timeperiod, and/or (2) arrange an alternate time frame for processing your originalrequest or modified request.  If you wish to pursue either of theseoptions, you must notify us. 

Sincerely, 
The FOIA Team, SSA

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request #2026-FOIA-00125 - Status 01-22-2026
communications.communication:
Dear Requester, 

We are continuing to process your request but require additional time.  No later than February 23, 2026 you will receive further correspondence from us. 

Please do not reply to this email correspondence, i.e., to this email address.  If you have an account withFOIAonline, you can correspond directly with us by logging into youraccount.  If you need to correspond with us regarding your FOIA requestbut do not have an account with FOIAonline, please send your correspondence to FOIA.Public.Liaison@ssa.gov.  The FOIA Public Liaison mailbox wascreated to assist the public with their FOIA requests, as well as to provideassistance to individuals requesting access to their own records under thePrivacy Act.  When you email thismailbox, we encourage you to limit the amount of personally identifiableinformation you provide in your email correspondence.  While the Social Security Administration (SSA)operates within a secure network, we have no control of the data we receivewhile it is in transit to or from our FOIA Public Liaison mailbox.  Please include your FOIA tracking number inyour email. (mailto:FOIA.Public.Liaison@ssa.gov) 
Sincerely, 
The FOIA Team, SSA

title: Georgia Records Request

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Georgia Records Request
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any and all documents that mention Cordavis possessed by staffers working for Department of Community Health's State Health Benefit Plan dated between August 2023 and the date of fulfillment of this request. The documents sought should include but not be limited to (introduced here only as illustrative examples) email communications, memoranda, meeting notes, letters, text messages, social media communications, video meeting recordings.

Definitions
 
“Any” and “all” mean all, each, any, and every.
 
“Document(s)” means any and all written, typed, printed, recorded or graphic matter, however produced, reproduced or stored, whether an original or a copy, and whether prepared, published or released by any person or entity, including but not limited to letters, reports, agreements, correspondence, intra-office or inter-office correspondence, telegrams, minutes or records of meetings, reports or summaries, expressions or statements, lists, drafts and revisions, invoices, receipts, original and preliminary notes, sketches, records, ledgers, contracts, bills of lading, bills, inventories, financial data, maps, memoranda, accounting and financial records, diaries, journals, calendars, statements, work papers, videotapes, photographs, pamphlets, brochures, advertisements, trade letters, press releases, drawings, recaps, tables, articles, summaries of conversations, computer cards, tapes, diskettes, or other means of electronically or magnetically maintaining information, and printouts. 
 
The term “document(s)” also includes electronically stored data from which information can be obtained either directly or by translation through detection devices or readers; any such document is to be produced in a reasonably legible and usable form. The term “document(s)” includes all drafts of a document and all copies that differ in any respect from the original, including any notation, underlining, marking, or information not on the original. The term also includes information stored in, or accessible through, computer or other information retrieval systems (including any computer archives or back-up systems), together with instructions and all other materials necessary to use or interpret such data compilations.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Steve Horn

communications.subject: Acknowledgement of Open Records Request # 64421
communications.communication:
The Georgia Department of Community Health (DCH) acknowledges receipt of your Open Records Act Request #64421 which was received by the Office of General Counsel on 11/10/2025. You have requested the following for date ranges 1/8/2023 to 11/10/2025 :

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any and all documents that mention Cordavis possessed by staffers working for Department of Community Health's State Health Benefit Plan dated between August 2023 and the date of fulfillment of this request. The documents sought should include but not be limited to (introduced here only as illustrative examples) email communications, memoranda, meeting notes, letters, text messages, social media communications, video meeting recordings.

Definitions

“Any” and “all” mean all, each, any, and every.

“Document(s)” means any and all written, typed, printed, recorded or graphic matter, however produced, reproduced or stored, whether an original or a copy, and whether prepared, published or released by any person or entity, including but not limited to letters, reports, agreements, correspondence, intra-office or inter-office correspondence, telegrams, minutes or records of meetings, reports or summaries, expressions or statements, lists, drafts and revisions, invoices, receipts, original and preliminary notes, sketches, records, ledgers, contracts, bills of lading, bills, inventories, financial data, maps, memoranda, accounting and financial records, diaries, journals, calendars, statements, work papers, videotapes, photographs, pamphlets, brochures, advertisements, trade letters, press releases, drawings, recaps, tables, articles, summaries of conversations, computer cards, tapes, diskettes, or other means of electronically or magnetically maintaining information, and printouts.

The term “document(s)” also includes electronically stored data from which information can be obtained either directly or by translation through detection devices or readers; any such document is to be produced in a reasonably legible and usable form. The term “document(s)” includes all drafts of a document and all copies that differ in any respect from the original, including any notation, underlining, marking, or information not on the original. The term also includes information stored in, or accessible through, computer or other information retrieval systems (including any computer archives or back-up systems), together with instructions and all other materials necessary to use or interpret such data compilations.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Steve Horn

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2127607/



Open Records has routed your request to the Division(s) who may have responsive records. Your request is being reviewed to preliminarily determine whether any records may exist that would be responsive. Under the Georgia Open Records Act (GORA) at O.C.G.A. §50-18-71(b)(1)(A), the statute provides that if responsive records exist but are unavailable within three business days, the agency is to provide the requester with a description of such records and a timeline for when the records will be available. At this time, we are determining whether responsive records exist. In the event such records do exist, we anticipate providing you with a timeline to respond to your request by 11/17/2025, and each week thereafter until such request is fulfilled. Should these records become available sooner they will be reviewed and supplied to you shortly there-after.

Please be advised that the Open Records Act has a provision that allows an agency to charge for the production of records. Pursuant to O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(d), DCH will provide a cost estimate and obtain your confirmation to pay the charges before finalizing the request if the cost to produce the records is in excess of $25.00.

Please continue to correspond with us via the Open Records Mailbox (openrecordsrequest@dch.ga.gov) so that we may better assist you by keeping all interested parties up to date on this matter.

Thank you for your patience. Please advise if I can be of further assistance.

title: IL SoS communications (Forest Park)

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: IL SoS communications (Forest Park)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

On September 5, 2025, Secretary of State Alexi Giannoulias issued a press release announcing that their audit uncovered that the Forest Park Police Department was sharing its license plate reader (LPR) data with the department of Customs and Border Protection. I am seeking email communications related to this issue. Specifically, I am seeking the following:

1. Emails with the Illinois Secretary of State's office
- Copies of all emails (including any attachments),
- Sent or received (including CC & BCC fields) by the Forest Park Mayor and Police Chief, 
- To or from any sender email address containing the domain @ilsos.gov,
- between 08/1/2025 and the 10/31/2025  (inclusive of those dates).

2. Emails with Motorola
- Copies of all emails (including any attachments),
- Sent or received (including CC & BCC fields) by the Forest Park Mayor and Police Chief, 
- To or from any sender email address containing the domain @motorolasolutions.com,  
- Between 08/1/2025 and the 10/31/2025  (inclusive of those dates).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

RTL

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: IL SoS communications (Forest Park)
communications.communication:
Hello – I will look into this FOIA.
Can you please confirm where the February 28 request was sent as it was not received to this email and it is not visible on the email below.
I will need an additional 5 days to gather relevant information. I will respond no later than March 18, 2026.
Please confirm receipt. The February 28 FOIA request was not received at our office. Only the FOIA today, March 4 was received.
Thank you.
Megan Roach
FOIA Officer

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: IL SoS communications (Forest Park)
communications.communication:
I have a clarification for this request. 
I am seeking the described emails sent or received (including CC & BCC fields) by the Forest Park Mayor *OR* the Police Chief. I am seeking the emails for one or the other people, not combined. 

I apologize if my initial request was unclear. Thank you for your assistance with this request.
RTL

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: IL SoS communications (Forest Park)
communications.communication:
Megan Roach, 
I apologize, I don't know where Muckrock routed the initial request. Regardless, I confirm that the receive date for your department was today, March 4 and I will patiently await a response.
For clarity, I will restate the request, inclusive of the adjustment I previously communicated. 
Thank you for your assistance with this request.

==== FOIA REQUEST ====
Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:
On September 5, 2025, Secretary of State Alexi Giannoulias issued a press release announcing that their audit uncovered that the Forest Park Police Department was sharing its license plate reader (LPR) data with the department of Customs and Border Protection. I am seeking email communications related to this issue. Specifically, I am seeking the following:

1. Emails with the Illinois Secretary of State's office
- Copies of all emails (including any attachments),
- Sent or received (including CC & BCC fields) by the Forest Park Mayor or Police Chief,
- To or from any sender email address containing the domain @ilsos.gov,
- between 08/1/2025 and the 10/31/2025 (inclusive of those dates).

2. Emails with Motorola
- Copies of all emails (including any attachments),
- Sent or received (including CC & BCC fields) by the Forest Park Mayor or Police Chief,
- To or from any sender email address containing the domain @motorolasolutions.com,
- Between 08/1/2025 and the 10/31/2025 (inclusive of those dates).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: IL SoS communications (Forest Park)
communications.communication:

Megan R.
Foia Officer

title: 2026 Brady List (Clallam County Prosecuting Attorney)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: 2026 Brady List (Clallam County Prosecuting Attorney)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

communications.subject: [External Message Added] Clallam County WA public records request #26-198
communications.communication:
Clallam County WA ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #26-198: Clallam County writes to acknowledge receipt of the public records request you submitted. Please accept this correspondence as our timely response to your request(s) as required under RCW 42.56.520. REQUEST: Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material. The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. RESPONSE: A search for records has been initiated and at this time we anticipate that, if located, either the requested records or a first installment of records should be available on or before February 19, 2026. This preliminary estimate takes into account, among other things, the need to locate and assemble the record(s) requested and determine whether any record(s) must be redacted.   If you have any questions, you can contact me by responding to this message or by calling me at the phone number below.  Sincerely, Jennifer Lindquist Deputy Public Records Officer Public Records, Information Technology Desk 360-417-2234 ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at Clallam County WA.

Technical support: See our help page

communications.communication:

title: CM Hollingsworth - AI Usage

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: CM Hollingsworth - AI Usage
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All text, images, and videos generated by CM Hollingsworth for City business using City-owned or personal generative AI software, as well as corresponding prompts used to generate them.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

communications.subject: Legislative Branch :: C249856-121625
communications.communication:
Dear Rose Terse, 
 
Welcome to the City of Seattle Public Records Request Center (PRRC). Your request was received on December 16, 2025 and is appended below. Please add seattle@mycusthelp.net to your email contacts/address book to ensure delivery of the record center emails to your Inbox. 
 
The reference number for this request is C249856-121625. You will see this number in the title of any communications about this request. 
 
You will hear from a Public Disclosure Officer within five business days regarding the status of your request.

title: Request to Navy for all 2025 film and TV collaborations

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request to Navy for all 2025 film and TV collaborations
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A list of all the films and television productions that the U.S. Navy has supported, in any capacity whatsoever, during the year 2025. Such a list, full or partial, maybe include, but should not limited to, entertainment liaison (ELO) reports or similar reports. I request that you search for these documents in any location where they might be located, including but not limited to the entertainment liaison offices and communications HQ.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

communications.subject: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: Legal Expenses Danbury, CT - Alves vs Giegler et al (Mayor's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Please provide a list of all billable time for City of Danbury Corporation Counsel and administrative staff as well as outside counsel billing received before October 25, 2023 in connection with trial, appellate, and supreme court litigation of the Alves vs Giegler matter. Feel free to request any clarification you may need

I also request that, if possible and pursuant to Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-212(d), fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest and not made for commercial gain. The requested documents will be made available to the public at MuckRock.com.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kevin Paige

communications.subject: FOIA Request
communications.communication:
This will acknowledge receipt of your letter received *January 13, 2026*,
wherein you requested information pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of
Information Act.


Please be advised that the City of Danbury is firmly committed to providing
prompt access to all records subject to disclosure under Connecticut
General Statutes §1-210, et. seq.  Accordingly, all documents will be
assembled and reviewed as quickly as reasonably possible in light of other
responsibilities and immediate commitments.  Although the City of Danbury
takes your request seriously and will respond as promptly as possible, we
reserve the right to petition the Freedom of Information Commission for a
declaratory ruling regarding requests that may be exempt from disclosure.



Please note, that there will be a charge of $.50 per page, and any amount
of over $10.00 must be paid in advance.



 If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact our office.


[image: photo]
*Kimberly Anderson*
Legal Administrative Assistant
Office of Corporation Counsel
City of Danbury

(203) 797-4518 ext. 7212 | k.anderson@danbury-ct.gov

(203) 796-8043 Fax

155 Deer Hill Avenue, Danbury, CT 06810

Mon - Tue: 8:30 am - 6:00 pm

wed - Thurs: 9:00 am - 6:00 pm

*City Hall is closed on Fridays.*

title: Cost to Acquire SS Simmons Victory and to Convert it to the USS Liberty (AGTR-5)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Cost to Acquire SS Simmons Victory and to Convert it to the USS Liberty (AGTR-5)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

In the 1960’s the US government acquired the SS Simmons Victory and converted it to the USS Liberty (AGTR-5).

We are requesting the documents related to the price the US government paid for the SS Simmons Victory and the cost the US government paid to convert the ship into the USS Liberty (AGTR-5).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joe Meadors

communications.subject: Fwd: Freedom of Information Act Request: Cost to Acquire SS Simmons Victory and to Convert it to the USS Liberty (AGTR-5)
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Meadors:

Thank you for submitting your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request to
the National Archives and Records Administration (NARA) via email to
foia@nara.gov.  We received your request on February 13, 2026.  Your
request states:

*In the 1960’s the US government acquired the SS Simmons Victory and
converted it to the USS Liberty (AGTR-5).  We are requesting the documents
related to the price the US government paid for the SS Simmons Victory and
the cost the US government paid to convert the ship into the USS Liberty
(AGTR-5).*

Our office handles FOIA and Privacy Act requests for NARA’s operational records
(records created by this agency).  Because your FOIA request is for records
that may be archival records (records created by or originated in a
different agency but that may have been transferred to the legal custody of
NARA), we are forwarding it by this email to our Office of Research
Services, Textual Reference (RR2RR) staff who handle FOIA requests for
textual archival records.

That office will assign your request a tracking number and respond directly
to you. In the future, if you need to follow up with that office directly,
please contact them at the address below, or electronically via email
(email is preferred).

National Archives and Records Administration
Research Services, Textual Reference Branch (RR2RR)
8601 Adelphi Road, Room 2400
College Park, MD 20740

Phone: 301-837-3091
Fax: 301-837-1752
Email: archives2reference@nara.gov

For your reference, a copy of your request is below.

Please visit our website at www.archives.gov/foia for further information
about making a FOIA request to NARA.  On our main website,
www.archives.gov, you
can learn more about the resources we offer to the public, and you can
access our online catalog.

Sincerely,

<https://www.archives.gov/>

  Ashley Bryan

  Government Information Specialist

  Operational FOIA and Access Division

  College Park, Maryland, 20740-6001

  301-837-3642

   <https://www.archives.gov/foia>   <foia@nara.gov>
<https://www.archives.gov/foia/contacts>
<https://www.archives.gov/foia/electronic-reading-room>

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following
records:

In the 1960’s the US government acquired the SS Simmons Victory and
converted it to the USS Liberty (AGTR-5).

We are requesting the documents related to the price the US government paid
for the SS Simmons Victory and the cost the US government paid to convert
the ship into the USS Liberty (AGTR-5).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and
this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform
me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer
the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or
CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I
look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business
days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joe Meadors

View request history, upload responsive documents, and report problems here:
https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2217060/

Filed via MuckRock.com
E-mail (Preferred): 204700-17185129@requests.muckrock.com

PLEASE NOTE OUR NEW ADDRESS
For mailed responses, please address (see note):
MuckRock News
DEPT MR 204700
263 Huntington Ave
Boston, MA 02115

PLEASE NOTE: This request is not filed by a MuckRock staff member, but is
being sent through MuckRock by the above in order to better track, share,
and manage public records requests. Also note that improperly addressed
(i.e., with the requester's name rather than "MuckRock News" and the
department number) requests might be returned as undeliverable.

communications.subject: Re: [26-27749] Freedom of Information Act Request - Joe Meadors
communications.communication:
Dear Joe Meadors,


This is in response to your Freedom of Information Act request dated
February 13, 2026 for records relating to the purchase and overhaul of the
SS Simmons Victory which occurred on the approximate dates of early 1960s.
Your request was received in this office, A2 Textual Reference, on the date
of February 19, 2026 and assigned FOIA case number 26-27749.


We have conducted a records review within Record Group 344: Records of
Naval Sea Systems Command and we have located records that may be
potentially relevant to your research. During our review we searched for
terms such as USS Liberty, AGTR-5, SS Simmons Victory, and variations
thereof. During our review we located Ship Departure Reports within Record
Group 344, Entry UD-04W 2, Series: Office of Management Services, Ship
Departure Reports, 1963-1968, File; AGTR 5. Additionally, we conducted a
review of Record Group 19: Records of the Navy's Bureau of Ships for
correspondence records, during our review we noted that correspondence
records for the Bureau of Ships dated 1962-1964 were not provided by the
Department of the Navy. We did not locate references to the SS Simmons
Victory or USS Liberty (AGTR-5) in the correspondence records dated 1961. The
materials you are requesting are already open for public viewing in our
research room at the National Archives in College Park, MD, located at 8601
Adelphi Road, College Park, MD. We are staffed to assist researchers with
their work by providing onsite reference services. Since there are no
access restrictions on these documents, there is no need to file a Freedom
of Information Act request with the National Archives.


We will be happy to make the records and their finding aids available to
you or your representative in the Textual Research Room (Room 2000) here at
the National Archives at College Park, Maryland. Please visit our website
for information about visiting the National Archives in College Park, MD
<https://www.archives.gov/college-park>, including how to schedule a
research visit. If you are unable to visit our research reading rooms you
may consider hiring a private researcher to conduct research on your
behalf. The scope of your research, combined with our staffing limitations
may make this the most efficient way to provide you with professional
research services. A list of private researchers familiar with National
Archives facilities is available on our Independent Researchers Available
for Hire <https://www.archives.gov/research/hire-help> webpage.


This concludes the processing of your request. We do not consider this a
denial of your FOIA request. If you are not satisfied with our action on
this request, your options for appeal and dispute resolution are attached.


We hope you find this information useful in conducting your research.

Sincerely,


JACOB A. HAYWOOD

Textual Reference Archives II Branch (RRAR)

National Archives at College Park, MD

title: Flock Safety Records

communications.subject: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: Flock Safety Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

1) I request internal training materials for the use of "Flock Safety" tools.

2) I request policies and procedures for the use of Flock tools

3) I request any sharing agreements signed with other agencies as well as department's sharing agreement it requires other agencies to conform to.

4) I request the web address of the department's "Flock Safety" transparency portal if activated. This is a free feature of Flock. This is not an add-on or analytics subscription.

5) Lastly, I request all available audit data from the "Flock" audit tools. This should include the organization audit, network audit, and sharing report.
    A. Organization Audit in Flock.
    The report should include data logged from the period of installation to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done by the City.

    B. Network Audit in Flock.
    The report should include data logged from the period of installation, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the City’s Flock network by any agency the City has shared with in the Flock System.

    C. Sharing report in Flock.
    The report should include flock networks shared with The City and networks The City is sharing its network with.

Flock maintains documentation on these reports. Flock documentation says the reports can be pulled in 31 day increments.  Flock also says that customers may want to contact support for requests longer than six months or for large accounts. If production of audit logs requires support from Flock, please declare such needs and have the City's point person on the Flock support ticket provide updates on their progress.  Please note that license plate numbers are public information or it would be unsafe to display them. Drivers would also be able to hide their plates in their trunk if they were meant to be private.  Officer usernames are similarly publicly available by many resources, such as news reports, court cases, case notes, and reports.

I request that files be supplied in their original electronic, machine readable format rather than being converted to any alternate formats. Where multiple formats are available for export, I request csv, xls, or other spreadsheet compatible formats.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael

Good Day,
I am following up on this request originally submitted on 1/23/2026.  To date, I have received no communication in response to this request.  This seems completely improper as the sunshine law statute allows three business days for a response to a request.  Under the law, production of records may take longer, but a detailed explanation for the delay and the earliest date and time records will be available must be shared.

With this being the case, I am requesting an immediate explanation for the delay in responding to this request and the earliest date and time the records I requested will be available.

Thank you,
Michael

communications.communication:
Tracy,
Crystal Higdon has been contacted numerous times as seen on this request.  This request was submitted on 1/23/26. Crystal Higdon has so far not responded.  Section 610.023.3, RSMo states that requests are to be responded to within three business days.  The law does not allow for a government agency to dictate the manner in which the request arrives.

To Whom It May Concern:

The annual budget report for the Navy's Public Affairs Office for the year 2019.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

title: Open Records Act Request for Records on Bar Eligibility, Immigration Status Requirements, and Investigations Involving Emory University law students (JD and LLM), including American domestic, F-1, J-1, and M-1 nonimmigrant students (SEVIS-tracked) Student

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Open Records Act Request for Records on Bar Eligibility, Immigration Status Requirements, and Investigations Involving Emory University law students (JD and LLM), including American domestic, F-1, J-1, and M-1 nonimmigran
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Subject: Open Records Act Request for Records on Bar Eligibility, Immigration Status Requirements, and Investigations Involving Emory University law students (JD and LLM), including American domestic, F-1, J-1, and M-1 nonimmigrant students (SEVIS-tracked) Students (2015–Present)
Dear OBA Custodian of Records:
Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, O.C.G.A. § 50-18-70 et seq., MuckRock requests this inspection and copies of the following public records:
1.	All applications, determinations, or denials of bar eligibility under Ga. Bar R. 4(c) for Emory University law students (JD and LLM), including American domestic, F-1, J-1, and M-1 nonimmigrant students (SEVIS-tracked) students, 2015–present, including those impacted by SEVIS terminations or immigration status.
2.	Correspondence, memos, or data between OBA and Emory University (Law School/ISSS), DHS/SEVP, or AG on bar access barriers for international students, including 2025 SEVIS effects.
3.	Aggregated, anonymized statistics on bar applicants from Emory/Georgia law schools denied due to status (undocumented, F-1, J-1, and M-1 nonimmigrant students (SEVIS-tracked)), 2015–present; outcomes/trends; including disparities data vs. ABA policies (e.g., Res. 108, 2017, on undocumented eligibility), empirical evidence of denials/waivers, and communications with Emory on transcript/funding issues.
4.	All OBA/State Bar/Supreme Court reports, statistics, or empirical evidence (e.g., case summaries, waiver denials) on undocumented/visa law student bar eligibility/fitness 2015–2025, including disparities vs. ABA policies (e.g., Res. 108, 2017; 2022 reforms), COVID waivers (Special Rule 2020-1), and communications with Emory on transcript/funding issues.
This disclosure is in the public interest because abrupt loss of lawful status without due process constitutes a severe liberty deprivation under Zadvydas v. Davis, 533 U.S. 678 (2001), governed by the Mathews v. Eldridge, 424 U.S. 319 (1976) balancing test, and recently recognized in Liu v. Noem (D.N.H. 2025) and Kuck Baxter v. DHS (N.D. Ga. 2025).
A complete fee waiver or reduction under O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(g) is requested because this request involves a matter of substantial public concern — the deprivation of due process and effective banishment of nonimmigrant students through no-notice SEVIS terminations (see Zadvydas v. Davis, 533 U.S. 678 (2001); Mathews v. Eldridge, 424 U.S. 319 (1976); Liu v. Noem, D.N.H. 2025; Kuck Baxter v. DHS, N.D. Ga. 2025).
Expedited processing is requested under O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(b)(2) due to imminent irreparable harm from loss of lawful status and ongoing litigation (Kuck Baxter v. DHS, N.D. Ga. 2025). 
Provide within 3 business days; electronic PDF format preferred. No search fees (Trammell v. Martin, 261 Ga. 1 (1991)).
If any fees are assessed despite the waiver request, please provide an itemized quote; payment will be made via prepaid gift card or U.S. Postal money order to preserve anonymity.
Thank you.
Sincerely,
MuckRock News on behalf of Anonymous Requester

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

neutralrequest47

communications.communication:
Good Morning!
The Office of Bar Admissions is an arm of the Supreme Court of Georgia which is part of the judicial branch of government and therefore are not included within the scope of the Georgia Open Records Act.  O.C.G.A. §§ 50-18-70(a), 50-14-1(a)(1); Fathers are Parents Too, Inc. v. Hunstein, 202 Ga. App. 716 (1992); 1979 Op. Att’y Gen 79-25.
Have a great week!


Rebecca S. Mick

Assistant Director

Office of Bar Admissions

Nathan Deal Judicial Center
330 Capitol Avenue, SE
Suite L200
Atlanta, GA  30334

404-656-2003

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Open Records Act Request for Records on Bar Eligibility, Immigration Status Requirements, and Investigations Involving Emory University law students (JD and LLM), including American domestic, F-1, J-1, and M-1 nonimmi
communications.communication:
Dear Rebecca S. Mick,
Thank you for your response dated December 9, 2025.
The position that the Office of Bar Admissions falls outside the general scope of the Georgia Open Records Act (“GORA”), O.C.G.A. §§ 50-18-70(a), 50-14-1(a)(1), as interpreted in Fathers Are Parents Too, Inc. v. Hunstein, 202 Ga. App. 716 (1992), and 1979 Op. Att’y Gen. 79-25 because Office of Bar Admissions operates as an arm of the Supreme Court of Georgia is, respectfully, irrelevant for the purposes of this request.
Respectfully, this request seeks administrative and statistical records, not adjudicative case files, and concerns matters of public administration rather than judicial decision-making.
Specifically, the request seeks:
1.	 Aggregated, anonymized statistical data
2.	Administrative correspondence regarding eligibility standards
3.	Policy guidance concerning immigration-status considerations
4.	Reports or empirical summaries concerning bar eligibility determinations
These categories involve the administrative operation of a professional licensing authority rather than confidential adjudicative deliberations in individual fitness cases.
Even where judicial entities are exempt from GORA as courts, Georgia law recognizes a distinction between adjudicative functions and administrative functions. Records reflecting administrative policy development, statistical reporting, or inter-agency communications are not judicial opinions or case files and therefore do not implicate judicial decisional independence.
Importantly, this is not a request for:
1.	Confidential character-and-fitness investigative files of specific applicants
2.	Deliberative materials of pending matters
3.	Personally identifying information of third parties
Rather, the request is limited to:
1.	Aggregate data
2.	Policy-level communications
3.	Administrative reports
4.	Non-case-specific determinations affecting categories of applicants
If the Office maintains that GORA does not apply in any respect, I respectfully request clarification as to whether:
1.	The Supreme Court of Georgia maintains any public-access mechanism for administrative records of the Office of Bar Admissions;
2.	Aggregated statistical data concerning eligibility determinations exists;
3.	Any portion of the request can be processed voluntarily in the interest of transparency.
If the denial rests on confidentiality under the Rules Governing Admission to the Practice of Law (Part F, Section 4), I respectfully note that confidentiality provisions protect individual applicant records and reputational interests; they do not preclude disclosure of anonymized statistical data or general policy communications.
I appreciate your time and look forward to your clarification regarding the proper procedural mechanism for obtaining administrative records, if not under GORA.
Sincerely,
neutralrequest47

communications.subject: Re: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Open Records Act Request for Records on Bar Eligibility, Immigration Status Requirements, and Investigations Involving Emory University law students (JD and LLM), including American domestic, F-1, J-1, and M-1 nonimmi
communications.communication:
Thank you for the clarification.  All of the information relating to your requests can be found on our website which you can access here<https://www.gabaradmissions.org/home>. Use the yellow ribbon at the top to explore different subjects such as Rules & Policies and Admission Types.  You can also use the search bar to pull up pages specific to your interest.  We do not create reports or have the statistical data you are requesting other than what is on our website.  All other documentation relating to this office is considered confidential by our Rules.  I hope this helps!




Rebecca S. Mick

Assistant Director

Office of Bar Admissions

Nathan Deal Judicial Center
330 Capitol Avenue, SE
Suite L200
Atlanta, GA  30334

404-656-2003

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Open Records Act Request for Records on Bar Eligibility, Immigration Status Requirements, and Investigations Involving Emory University law students (JD and LLM), including American domestic, F-1, J-1, and M-1 nonimmi
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Mick,
Subject: Clarification Regarding Administrative Records
Thank you for your prompt response.
I understand the Office’s position that it operates as an arm of the Supreme Court of Georgia and therefore may not be subject to the Georgia Open Records Act.
My request, however, concerns administrative and statistical records rather than individual applicant files or adjudicative deliberations.
To clarify, I respectfully ask:
1.	Whether aggregated statistical data concerning eligibility determinations exists in any format;
2.	Whether any annual administrative summaries or reports are maintained;
3.	Whether any public-facing policy memoranda address immigration-status considerations in bar eligibility.
If the Office maintains that no such aggregated or administrative records exist beyond the website materials referenced, I respectfully request written confirmation clarifying whether such records do not exist, are not maintained in the ordinary course of business, or are being withheld pursuant to a specific Rule or policy, with citation to the precise provision relied upon.
My intent is not to obtain confidential applicant information, but rather to understand the general administrative framework governing eligibility categories.
Thank you for your time and consideration.
Sincerely,
neutralrequest47

communications.subject: Re: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Open Records Act Request for Records on Bar Eligibility, Immigration Status Requirements, and Investigations Involving Emory University law students (JD and LLM), including American domestic, F-1, J-1, and M-1 nonimmi
communications.communication:
Hello,

Thank you for your follow-up inquiry. The Office of Bar Admissions operates as an arm of the Supreme Court of Georgia. Unfortunately, we are unable to fulfill your request for documents outside of those previously referenced by the former Assistant Director because the judicial branch falls outside the scope of Georgia's Open Records Act. See O.C.G.A. §§ 50-18-70(a); 50-14-1; Fathers are Parents Too v. Hunstein, 202 Ga. App. 716 (1992); 1979 Op. Att'y Gen. 79-25. We do not maintain public documents outside of the scope of those made accessible to the public through our website. In this regard, you can find the policy of the Board to Determine Fitness of Bar Applicants as it relates to immigration status here<https://www.gabaradmissions.org/policy-statement> (under Part F).

I hope that information is helpful to you.

With Best Regards,

Kevin


[cid:954a8a4e-e5f8-45f8-8b64-944464a09e08]    Kevin C. Wilson

    Assistant Director, Office of Bar Admissions

    Supreme Court of Georgia

    330 Capitol Avenue SE, Atlanta, Georgia 30334

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Santa Rosa County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Santa Rosa County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Utah HHS response

communications.subject: Government Records Access and Management Act Request: Utah HHS response
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Government Records Access and Management Act, I hereby request the following records:

Communications and correspondence with the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services regarding the Aug. 26, 2025, HHS letter requesting the Utah Department of Health and  Human Services remove content from the state's Personal Responsibility Education Program curricula and program materials. For reference, the letter can be found here: https://acf.gov/sites/default/files/documents/main/08.26.25-Combined-PREP-Letters.pdf.

I also request internal communications between any Utah Department of Health and  Human Services employees about the aforementioned letter. If the content changes were made, I request a copy of the material before it was changed as well as a copy of the material after it was changed.

I additionally request that, if possible, fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, processed by a representative of the news media/press and is made in the process of news gathering and not for commercial usage.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ileana Garnand

communications.subject: GRAMA Response
communications.communication:
Please see the attached GRAMA response.

Thank you,

Dianna Sanchez
GRAMA Coordinator
Utah Department of Health & Human Services
385-459-8588
diannas@utah.gov
https://hs.utah.gov/



Please note Utah has a very broad public records law. Most written
communication to or from our state employees regarding state business are
public records available to the public and media upon request. Your email
communication may be subject to public disclosure.

-- 
*************** IMPORTANT MESSAGE ***************
This message, including 
any attachments, may contain confidential information intended for a 
specific individual and purpose, and is protected by law.  If you are not 
the intended recipient, delete this message, including from trash, and 
notify me by telephone or email.


If you are not the intended recipient, 
any distributions or copying of this message, or the taking of any action 
based on its content is strictly prohibited.

title: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (New Jersey Secretary of State)

communications.subject: New Jersey Open Public Records Act Request: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (New Jersey Secretary of State)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Jersey Open Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

From January 20, 2025 to the present:

1. All correspondence, emails, letters, and written communications between the agency and the U.S. Department of Justice, including but not limited to the Civil Rights Division, the Office of the Attorney General, and the Executive Office for Immigration Review, relating to voter registration data, voter files, voter rolls, or voter list maintenance.

2. All internal communications within the agency (including emails, memoranda, and briefing materials) discussing, referencing, or responsive to any DOJ request for voter registration data or any proposed or executed memorandum of understanding with the DOJ regarding voter data.

3. Any lists, spreadsheets, databases, or other records received from the DOJ identifying specific voters for removal, investigation, or further review.

4. All records relating to the engagement of any third-party contractor in connection with DOJ voter data requests or voter list maintenance activities conducted pursuant to any agreement with the DOJ.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

communications.subject: RE: New Jersey Open Public Records Act Request: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (New Jersey Secretary of State)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I wanted to follow up on the following New Jersey Open Public Records Act request, copied below, and originally submitted on Feb. 19, 2026. Please let me know when I can expect to receive a response.

Thanks for your help, and let me know if further clarification is needed.

communications.subject: Government Records Request to New Jersey Department of State (DOJ Voter Data Agreements (New Jersey Secretary of State))
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

The New Jersey Department of State is in receipt of your Government Records Request submitted to Feedback.SOS@sos.nj.gov.  You requested in relevant part:

1. Any and all memoranda of understanding (MOUs), agreements, contracts, or similar instruments between the state and the U.S. Department of Justice relating to the provision of voter registration data, voter files, or voter roll information, from January 20, 2025 to the present.
2. Any draft, unsigned, proposed, or rejected versions of such agreements.
3. Any amendments, addenda, or modifications to such agreements.
This request includes but is not limited to any document described or labeled as a "memorandum of understanding" or contract relating to voter data, voter list maintenance, or voter roll verification.

Please see the attached record which is responsive to your request for a draft Memorandum of Understanding regarding the State's voter registration data.  There are no other responsive records maintained, held, or otherwise received by the New Jersey Department of State to your request.

This will officially close out your Government Records Request.

Sincerely,

    New Jersey Department of State OPRA

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Luzerne County Sheriff)

communications.subject: Pennsylvania Right to Know Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Luzerne County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Pennsylvania Right to Know Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: University of Minnesota Police LRAD use against protestors

communications.subject: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: University of Minnesota Police LRAD use against protestors
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

In February 2026, multiple independent, local, and national news outlets published photographs and reporting regarding so-called “noise demonstrations” occurring on or near the University of Minnesota campus. Reporting and photographic evidence indicate that the University of Minnesota Police Department (UMPD) responded to several of these events and, in at least some instances, deployed a Long-Range Acoustical Device (LRAD). Multiple publicly available images show an LRAD device connected to a UMPD vehicle.

Accordingly, I request the following records:
1. LRAD Acquisition Records: All purchase orders, invoices, receipts, contracts, grant documentation, vendor correspondence, and related procurement records for any LRAD devices currently or previously in the possession of UMPD, including but not limited to devices manufactured by LRAD Corporation / Genasys, and including models such as the LRAD 500X.
--
2. LRAD Training and Operational Materials: All manuals, training documents, lesson plans, presentations (including PowerPoint or equivalent), standard operating procedures, quick-reference guides, safety warnings, or other instructional materials that describe, govern, or advise UMPD personnel on the operation, deployment, configuration, volume settings, warnings, or tactical use of LRAD devices.
--
3. Policies Governing Use of Force and Crowd Control: All current and historical UMPD policies, directives, or guidelines governing: Use of force, Crowd control or protest response, Deployment of less-lethal or acoustic devices, Escalation or authorization requirements for specialized equipment.
--
4. Deployment and After-Action Records (February 2026)
Any after-action reports, incident reports, operational summaries, or internal reviews referencing LRAD use during protests or demonstrations in February 2026 (Feb 1 - Feb 9), including records that identify: Date(s) and location(s) of deployment, Authorizing personnel, Purpose and duration of use.
--
If any portion of this request is unclear or requires clarification to facilitate a timely response, I am happy to confer.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Alan J. Segal

communications.subject: Thank you for contacting the Department of Public Safety Re: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: University of Minnesota Police LRAD use against protestors
communications.communication:
If this is an emergency or an urgent concern, please call 9-1-1.  If you witness or have a situation that needs immediate attention, please call 9-1-1. 

This account is not actively monitored.  Emails to the University of Minnesota Department of Public Safety are normally responded to within 2-3 business days.  If you require assistance sooner, please contact us at 612 624-COPS (2677). 

If you are emailing to report a crime, please either call us at 612 624-2677 or visit our department at 505 Washington Ave SE to file a police report.  Police reports are not accepted at this email address. 

For media inquiries, please contact University Public Relations at unews@umn.edu. (mailto:unews@umn.edu) 

Thank you.

communications.subject: Re: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: University of Minnesota Police LRAD use against protestors
communications.communication:
You can submit a data request at umn.nextrequest.com.

Sincerely,
Shannon

communications.subject: Your University of Minnesota public records request #26-268 has been submitted.
communications.communication:
University of Minnesota ************************************************************************ Your record request #26-268 has been submitted successfully. Dear Requester: Thank you for using our online records management portal to request public records from the University of Minnesota. You can monitor the progress of your request by clicking on the blue button within this notification. Adding this email address for the Data Request Center will help to ensure future request related emails do not end up in your spam folder. You will receive an email when your request has been completed.  Our goal is to respond to as many requests from as many requesters as quickly as possible. To achieve that goal while fielding a high volume of requests, we prioritize based on the complexity of the request and/or volume of data requested. Targeted requests are typically completed more quickly. If your request is large and/or complex, we will contact you to discuss opportunities to focus your request and allow us to respond to it more efficiently. Again, thank you for using the Data Request Center. Sincerely,  Laurie Beyer-Kropuenske MGDPA Responsible Authority Chief Data Practices Compliance Officer ************************************************************************ Reply to this email or sign in to contact University of Minnesota.

Change your email settings | Visit our help center

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Olivia James.

RE: Records Request - Southwood Apartments, Richmond, VA – Environmental Site Assessment

Good day,

In keeping with the statutory requirements for service, please provide your physical address when submitting future requests.

Pursuant to the matter listed above, and in accordance with Code of Virginia § 2.2-3704 (of the Virginia Freedom of Information Act), please find the following, as outlined in your request, as it exists in the Department of Planning and Development Review (PDR).
As of this writing, beyond the attached, there exists no violation of applicable building or zoning codes for the cited address(es).
The City of Richmond provides an online tool for (chronological) information related to permitting (proposed and issued).  It is conducted on its proprietary Online Permit Portal (OPP).  Using this tool, simply enter the address number, direction (if applicable) and name, EXCLUDING (address) suffixes – ST, RD, AV, WAY, etc.  Click, ‘SEARCH’ , and all available information will populate the bottom of your screen (to the extent it exists).  This is a ‘real-time’ tool, which extends from 1991, to the point of this writing.

https://energov.richmondgov.com/EnerGov_Prod/SelfService/richmondvaprod#/home<https://url.avanan.click/v2/r01/___https:/energov.richmondgov.com/EnerGov_Prod/SelfService/richmondvaprod%23/home___.YXAzOnBhcnRuZXJlc2k6YTpvOjBmMjQyYTcyODY5MDZhMzhkNWQxOGRhMTBlMTUxZTk5Ojc6MTYyNTpiM2U2NDgyNDc5MWExODU0ZGEzM2ExYmVjY2Q4ZTA2MWMwYjIyZDQ4NTdjZTQ2YjAwMmNhZjA2N2E3ZmJlNGVlOmg6VDpO>  --  COR – Online Permit Portal

Please note: Typically, Phase I Environmental Assessments, relative to storage tanks and related matters (possibly spills, cleanups, testing, etc. for any historic property uses) reside under the purview of the Department of Fire – Office of the Fire Marshal for the City Of Richmond.
Items requested related to water lines, and sewer lines or laterals (and other related matters) will reside with the Departments Utilities, and Public Works, respectively.
Date of initial construction is provided online by the official holder of that record, (from) the Office of the Assessor of Real Estate - https://www.rva.gov/assessor-real-estate<https://url.avanan.click/v2/r01/___https:/www.rva.gov/assessor-real-estate___.YXAzOnBhcnRuZXJlc2k6YTpvOjBmMjQyYTcyODY5MDZhMzhkNWQxOGRhMTBlMTUxZTk5Ojc6MTk2NzpmMzFkNDhlY2Q2MWQ5NmFhYjJjNjAyZWYxZGZiOTA5OGVkMmE4YzM1YjEzYWE1MjcwNWMzOTg5ZWUyODE4ODFlOmg6VDpO> .
Matters outlined in your request related to Zoning (Variances, and otherwise) research will reside outside of the FOIA tool and will take their form through a request of a Zoning Confirmation Letter (ZCL) from the Office of Zoning Administration.
The cost of this facilitation is $126.81 (= $ 28.18 p/h X ½ hr. X 9 sites).  Remittance is due, payable to the City of Richmond, within ten (10) business days ‘after’ receipt of these fulfillment records (@ 900 East Broad ST, Rm 110, Richmond, VA 23219).
As always, we welcome this opportunity to assist you.
Sincerely,

Isaac Marks
Freedom of Information Officer
Bureau of Permits and Inspections
[cid:image001.jpg@01DC3DEA.C23DE4B0]
900 E. Broad Street, Room 110
Richmond, Virginia 23219

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Bossier Parish Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: Louisiana Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Bossier Parish Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Louisiana Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe
